questions.question: Explain two advantages of using Linux on Raspberry Pi for IoT applications compared to other operating systems.
keyPoints: Open source nature allows free use and modification
Extensive software ecosystem and package management
Strong security features and community support
Excellent hardware support for Raspberry Pi peripherals

questions.question: Describe the process of setting up a Python IoT application to run automatically at boot on a Raspberry Pi, including security considerations.
keyPoints: Creating a systemd service file in /etc/systemd/system/
Configuring the service with appropriate execution commands and user permissions
Using systemctl to enable and start the service
Implementing proper logging for debugging and monitoring
Security considerations: using non-privileged user accounts, securing configuration files
Testing the service functionality and automatic startup

questions.question: Explain the Linux filesystem hierarchy and identify at least four directories that are particularly important for IoT applications on Raspberry Pi.
keyPoints: /etc/ for system configuration files
/home/pi/ for user project files and scripts
/var/log/ for system and application logs
/sys/class/gpio/ for hardware GPIO access via sysfs
/dev/ for device files representing hardware

questions.question: Describe how you would deploy and monitor a Python-based IoT application as a systemd service on Raspberry Pi, including the service file structure, commands used, and logging setup.
keyPoints: Create a service file in /etc/systemd/system/ with Unit, Service, and Install sections
Configure ExecStart to point to the Python script with full paths
Set Restart=always for automatic recovery from crashes
Use systemctl daemon-reload, enable, and start commands
Monitor with journalctl -u service_name -f for real-time logs
Implement Python logging module for application-specific log files
